Bech disappointed as a rookie, especially considering the poor WR depth chart for the Raiders. He went 20-224-0 in 2025 and will be paired with a. new coaching staff in 2026. Perhaps Bech can still develop and earn the slot job next season with a rookie QB, so he is worth stashing.

Thompson played at Oklahoma and Texas early in his career, but barely saw the field. He made the jump to Mississippi State in 2025 and produced, going 57-1054-6 on the season. Thompson is a big play threat who could challenge to be the fastest WR at the NFL Combine this year. His blazing speed helps him overcome his 5'9, 170 pound frame.

Despite missing several games, Robinson set a career high with 8 rushing scores in 2024. His receiving usage predictably declined, though, while 2025 turned up a move to San Francisco. After playing a handcuff role to CMC, Robinson reached free agency in March.

Similar to Tyrone Tracy, Brashard Smith spent most of his collegiate career as a WR. After transferring to SMU from Miami, Smith changed positions to RB and was the central part of their offensive success. Lining up both wide and in the backfield, he is an intriguing weapon with great short-area quickness and ability. We saw flashes of that in Year 1, although the Chiefs appeared hesitant to truly unleash him. Smith will remain no more than a part-timer moving forward, especially after the signing of Kenneth Walker.

Neal broke out as a true freshman on a terrible Kansas team, rushing for 707 yards and eight TDs. Since then, he tallied three straight 1,000-yard seasons, including 1,266 yards and 16 TDs in 2024. He also caught at least 20 passes in each of those three seasons. As a rookie, Neal looked just OK in late-season work with Alvin Kamara sidelined. New Orleans then added a clear-cut RB1 in Travis Etienne.

Allar was a potential top-100 pick in the 2025 NFL Draft but opted to go back to school. That led to only 8 TDs and 3 INTs in 6 games before Allar broke his ankle. He has some interesting traits, including a bit of rushing upside. But, Allar has never pulled together a full college season and may be overdrafted based on his physical tools.

Johnson's rookie year proved disastrous. After arriving in Round 3, the Iowa standout spread only 28 carries (for 69 yards) across 10 games. He simply wasn't able to earn a role alongside Jaylen Warren and Kenneth Gainwell. We then saw how little the team believes in Johnson, as Rico Dowdle arrived on a two-year deal. Note that Warren is signed through the 2027 season.

Tillman dealt with multiple injuries in 2025, only playing in 13 games. His 21-270-2 stat line was a step back from 2024, but the Browns horrible passing offense may be to blame. A new offensive system and QB could benefit Tillman in 2026.

Tucker broke out in his third season going 57-696-5. Tucker's 2025 was an improvement in catches, yardage and TDs from 2024. His deep speed made him a downfield option working from the slot. He will have to fend off WRs like Jack Bech and possibly new additions in 2026, but Tucker is a young dynasty stash.

Nussmeier showcased a strong arm in 2024 at LSU, throwing for 4,052 yards with 29 TDs and 12 INTs. He dealt with an abdominal strain all season in 2025, causing him to miss the final four games. He is a pure pocket passer who can get the ball out quickly and likes taking risks, but he may be tough for NFL teams to trust before Day 3.

Between his leg fracture in 2023 and dislocated kneecap and torn ACL in 2024, Dell did not play at all in 2025. His career is in jeopardy, but if Dell can somehow get back healthy, he could be a sneaky add on deep dynasty rosters.

Wright was one of the speediest RBs in the 2024 class. According to The Athletic’s Bruce Feldman, Wright hit a Tyreek Hill-like 23.6 MPH in practice. The three-year Volunteer averaged a ridiculous 7.4 yards per carry in 2023, with 19 of his 136 attempts going for 15+ yards (per PFF). He showed out at the Combine with a 4.38 forty time and a 38-inch vertical. Miami provided an ideal landing spot for creative usage, but 2024 turned up light work behind De'Von Achane. The same trend surfaced in '25, while Miami also added rookie RB Ollie Gordon. We'll see how this backfield depth shakes out under a new coaching staff.

Blue was expected to be the next big Texas RB after Sophomore CJ Baxter went down with an injury. However, struggles with his vision early in 2024 led to a pass-catching role for the former four-star recruit. Blue ran a 4.38 at the NFL Combine despite nursing a torn muscle in his groin. His 4.25 Pro Day time may be more accurate to his actual speed. As the first RB drafted by the Cowboys, Blue carried some appeal entering the '25 season. But he barely saw the field and watched as Javonte Williams rebounded in a major way. Dallas then re-signed Williams to a three-year contract, putting a lid on Blue's dynasty value.
Mayer flashed his talent on a poor Raiders offense when Brock Bowers was injured. There's just not projectable fantasy value here, at least for 2026. Mayer is slated for free agency in 2027.

Miller never had a breakout season for Alabama, topping out at 504 rushing yards in 2025. His lack of production is concerning, but Miller's skill set as a stout RB who moves nimbly in short spaces makes him an attractive depth RB in the NFL. He has soft hands that weren't showcased enough, too.

Payton is a small school prospect with only one year of starting. He led a successful NDSU offense throwing for 2,719 yards with 16 TDs and 4 INTs. He is a big QB who can take hits and keeps his eyes downfield, but Payton will be a developmental prospect.

Davis' college tape featured a nice combo of power, patience, and vision. The Jets' landing spot just hasn't been a friendly one, especially with Breece Hall returning on the franchise tag. Davis will also be pushed by third-year RB Braelon Allen.

Austin worked as the No. 2 WR behind D.K. Metcalf in 2025. He flashed when Metcalf was out due to suspension. He is unlikely to be a long term answer, but he should stick on the Steelers with a role in 2026 and beyond.

Allen had his background looked into deeply by the NFL after having an assault charge dismissed and expunged from his record. With that aside, he exited college with two straight 1,000-yard rushing seasons and 102 receptions over that period. His multi-faceted skill set makes him a dynasty sleeper, even after a quiet rookie season.

Richardson lost a camp competition to Daniel Jones in 2025. Then, when Jones suffered an Achilles tear, Richardson wasn't able to play due to a freak eye injury. He isn't a lock to remain on the roster in 2026.

Washington got a shot to play when Tyreek Hill went down with an injury. He went 46-317-3 in 2025, earning the slot role. With a new head coach next year, Washington's role is unknown, but he is worth keeping on the roster to see how the offseason goes.

Allen returned to the Chargers and had a strong early season. He ended up going 81-777-4 in 2025, but will turn 34 in the offseason. Allen may have one year left in his NFL career, but will likely have to take a back-up role. It is unlikely he sticks as a fantasy starter in 2026, and certainly is nearing retirement.

Thornton had plenty of preseason hype, but that didn't come to fruition in his rookie year. Thornton went 10-135-0 during the 2025 season despite often playing an outside role early in the season. Perhaps a new coaching staff can maximize Thornton's size and speed.

Bigsby wrapped up a productive career at Auburn with 540-2,903-25 (35 games). He tested as an average athlete at the Combine, however, and his struggles carried over into a mistake-filled first season. Bigsby looked like a different back in 2024, showing the power and burst that made him an attractive prospect. A wrench was thrown into his 2025 plans, as the Jags traded him to Philly over the summer. He’ll remain in a backup/handcuff role.

Mooney took a step back in 2025 going 32-443-1 following up his nearly 1,000 yard season in 2024. Mooney battled injuries throughout the season, but he clearly lost a step in his age-28 season. The Falcons may address the WR position this offseason pushing Mooney further down the depth chart.
